relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Israel moved toward Egypt through Beersheba, offering sacrifice to the God of Isaac.
In night vision, the Divine called Jacob twice, and he answered: Here I am.
Fear was met with promise: Egypt would become the womb of a great nation.
The Divine promised to go down with him and bring him up again.
Jacob was carried in Pharaoh’s wagons with children and women.
All the seed gathered from Canaan moved toward Egypt.
Sons, daughters, grandsons, and granddaughters came down together.
The names of Israel’s house are remembered.
Reuben’s sons are named.
Simeon’s sons are named.
Levi’s sons are named.
Judah’s line is named, including Perez and Zerah.
Issachar’s sons are named.
Zebulun’s sons are named.
Leah’s household count is gathered.
Gad’s sons are named.
Asher’s sons and Serah are remembered.
Zilpah’s household is counted.
Rachel’s sons are Joseph and Benjamin.
Joseph’s Egyptian-born sons are Manasseh and Ephraim.
Benjamin’s sons are named.
Rachel’s house is counted.
Dan’s son is named.
Naphtali’s sons are named.
Bilhah’s house is counted.
Sixty-six persons came from Jacob’s body into Egypt.
With Joseph’s sons, Jacob’s house in Egypt is remembered as seventy.
Judah was sent ahead toward Joseph and Goshen.
Joseph met Israel, fell on his neck, and wept long.
Israel could now face death, having seen Joseph alive.
Joseph prepared to present his family to Pharaoh.
They would be named as shepherds and keepers of livestock.
Pharaoh would ask their work.
Their shepherd identity would place them in Goshen, apart within Egypt.
